_comment: Tier-2 routing cases. 'positive' prompts must rank the skill in the top-K by lexical overlap with its description; 'negative' prompts must NOT rank it first. Vocabulary is drawn from what a user would actually say. A miss means the skill's description is missing user vocabulary (positive) or is over-broad (negative) — the two failure modes that dominate real trigger bugs.

positive: let's add a feature to the app
can we build a new module for this project
I'm thinking about supporting offline mode
negative: the login button is throwing an error

positive: a test is failing with an unexpected exception
the app crashes and the output is wrong
there is a flaky CI job and a performance regression
negative: let's build a brand new feature from scratch

positive: I'm about to write production code for a new feature
add a mock and a test utility to this behavior change
the tests are passing and it works, we're done
before committing and opening a PR confirm this is fixed
review this branch and PR diff before merging
review the set of changes since main
negative: write a failing test for this function

positive: a small in-scope tweak to an already-shipped feature
recolor and copy edit this existing spec'd behavior
how does this library API actually behave
look into what the RFC standard says and write it up with citations
interview me to stress-test this plan before we build
grill my feature idea to draw out the decisions
the requirements changed mid-implementation and the spec has drifted
check-trace is failing in CI and the specs are out of sync
set up an isolated git worktree before editing
implementation on the branch is complete, should I merge or open a PR or discard
write the user stories and EARS acceptance criteria for this feature
an approved tasks.md exists, time to implement and build out the plan
drive the finished feature through the running system before merging
